classes.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.BaseBinder

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.BaseBinder.get_by_id
documentation: Obtains an object with a specific id

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.BaseBinder.safe_get_by_id
documentation: Like get_by_id, only returning None if the object could not be found

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.BaseBinder.get
documentation:
Finds exactly one object matching criteria. Raises {class}`.ObjectNotFound` if not found,
{class}`.TooManyObjectsFound` if more than one is found

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.BaseBinder.safe_get
documentation: Like {meth}`.get`, only returns `None` if no objects were found

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.BaseBinder.sample
documentation: Chooses a random sample out of those returned. Raises ValueError if there are not enough items

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.BaseBinder.choose
documentation: Chooses a random element out of those returned. Raises ObjectNotFound if none were returned

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.BaseBinder.safe_choose
documentation: Like choose, but returns None when not found

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.BaseBinder.to_list
documentation:
Returns the entire set of objects as a Python list

:::{caution}
Queries are lazy by default to avoid heavy API calls and repetitive page
requests. Using `to_list` will forcibly iterate and fetch all objects, which might
be a very big collection. This can cause issues like slowness and memory exhaustion
:::
Binds a specific type to a system.

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.MonomorphicBinder.get_by_id_lazy
documentation:
Obtains an object with a specified id *without* checking if it exists or querying it on the way.

This is useful assuming the next operation is a further query/update on this object.

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.MonomorphicBinder.get_mutable_fields
documentation: Returns a list of all mutable fields for this object type

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.MonomorphicBinder.find
documentation:
Queries objects according to predicates. Can receive arguments in two possible forms:

1. Direct keyword arguments, for filtering for equality:

   ```
   system.volumes.find(size=GiB)
   ```

2. Complex predicates, using the comparators:

   ```
   system.volumes.find(system.volumes.fields.size > GiB)
   system.volumes.find(Q.name != 'some_name')
   ```

```
returns

Lazy query result object.
```

:::{seealso}
{class}`infinisdk.core.object_query.ObjectQuery`
:::

methods.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.TypeBinder.create
documentation: Creates an object on the system

classes.name: infinisdk.core.type_binder.SubObjectTypeBinder
documentation:
This class provides a TypeBinder for an InfiniBoxSubObject

"find", and others.

classes.name: infinisdk.core.api.special_values.Autogenerate
documentation:
Designates a value that should be autogenerated upon request. The argument to the constructor is a
template string. When formatted, these fields can be used:

- ordinal: the number of times this template has been used already in this session
- time: the current time, as a floating point number
- timestamp: an integral value designating the current time (milliseconds)
- uuid: a unique identifier generated from uuid4
- short_uuid: a short (hopefully) unique identifier
- prefix: a prefix added to all generated values, will be added to template unless defined in the given template

documentation: pylint: disable=abstract-method

classes.name: infinisdk.core.bindings.ListOfRelatedObjectIDsBinding
documentation:
Binding for translating list objects info (dictionaries) to list of objects
API value = [1, 2]
InfiniSDK will return:
value = [<object id=1>, <object id=2>]

classes.name: infinisdk.core.bindings.ListOfRelatedObjectBinding
documentation:
Binding for translating list objects info (dictionaries) to list of objects
API value = [{'id': 1, k: v, ...}, {'id': 2, k: v, ...}]
InfiniSDK will return:
value = [<object id=1>, <object id=2>]

classes.name: infinisdk.core.bindings.ListOfRelatedComponentBinding
documentation:
Binding for translating list components info (dictionaries) to list of objects
API value = [{'index': 1, k: v, ...}, {'index': 2, k: v, ...}]
InfiniSDK will return:
value = [<component index=1>, <component index=2>]

classes.name: infinisdk.core.bindings.ListToDictBinding
documentation:
Binding for simple api quirks, where api expects the following:
[ { "name" : value1 }, { "name" : value2 } ]
InfiniSDK will use a simple list of strings:
[ value1, value2 ]

functions.name: infinisdk.core.utils.resolvers.schedules_resolver
documentation:
Returns a schedule with id=api_value.
This is for cases when you can't get
to the schedule from its parent policy,
e.g. when dealing with SG and its snapshots
which don't get the policy copied from the
MASTER entity.

functions.name: infinisdk.core.utils.query_utils.add_comma_separated_query_param
documentation:
```pycon
>>> str(add_comma_separated_query_param("http://a.com/a/b/c", "sort", "a"))
'http://a.com/a/b/c?sort=a'
>>> str(add_comma_separated_query_param("http://a.com/a/b/c?sort=a", "sort", "b"))
'http://a.com/a/b/c?sort=a%2Cb'
>>> str(add_comma_separated_query_param("http://a.com/a/b/c", "sort", ("a", "b")))
'http://a.com/a/b/c?sort=a%2Cb'
```

methods.name: infinisdk.core.api.api.API.set_auth
documentation:
Sets the username and password under which operations will be performed

Can be used both with a tuple argument or with two arguments (username, password):

```pycon
>>> system.api.set_auth(('username', 'password'))
>>> system.api.set_auth('username', 'password')
```

methods.name: infinisdk.core.api.api.API.get_auth
documentation: Returns a tuple of the current username/password used by the API

methods.name: infinisdk.core.api.api.API.get_auth_context
documentation:
Changes the API authentication information for the duration of the context:

```pycon
>>> with system.api.get_auth_context('username', 'password'):
...     ... # execute operations as 'username'
```

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.active_directory.ActiveDirectoryDomains.create
documentation:
Join an active directory domain

```
param domain

the domain to join

type domain

str

param org_unit

the organization unit

type org_unit

str

param preferred_ips

a list of ips

type preferred_ips

list[str]

param username

the username for the domain

type username

str

param password

the password for the domain

type password

str

param tenant

the tenant object

type tenant



param unix_services_enabled

indicates if RFC2307 Unix Services are to be used when joined to Active Directory

type unix_services_enabled

bool

param uid_attribute_name

The name of the AD attribute that provides the numeric UID of a user principal

type uid_attribute_name

str

param gid_attribute_name

The name of the AD attribute that provides the numeric GID of a user principal

type gid_attribute_name

str

param max_translated_groups

The maximum number of groups translated per user via RFC2307 Unix Services

type max_translated_groups

int

returns

Dictionary with fields: "tenant_id", "domain", "org_unit", "preferred_ips", "unix_services_enabled", "uid_attribute_name", "gid_attribute_name", "max_translated_groups"

rtype

dict
```

classes.name: infinisdk.infinibox.system_object.InfiniBoxSubObject
documentation:
Adds support for subobjects when the subobject appears in the URL api path
at most 1 level deep. E.g. "api/rest/father/23/son/1".
This means that every InfiniBoxSubObject
must have an is_parent_field=True Field argument for the parent field.
There can only be exactly 1 parent field.
The parent field should be the parent object, in the example above it will
be the "father" object.
So in order to create a "Son" object you'll create it inheriting from
this InfiniBoxSubObject, and setting is_parent_field=True for the parent ("father") Field
which you'll have in the child object.
The "father" field will be of type "Father".
The URL_PATH of this object is defined relative to the parent object,
so in the example above it will be "son".

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.volume.VolumesBinder.create_group_snapshot
documentation:
Creates multiple snapshots with a single consistent point-in-time, returning the snapshots


methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.compatibility.Compatibility.has_require_encryption
documentation:
Unlike most of the has_<feature_name> methods this method does not follow
this convention since it was added for a different reason:
In the API, the `Share` field name 'require_encryption'
was changed to 'encryption' in versions 8.1 and above.
This method was added in order to not break backward compatibility.

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.compatibility.Compatibility.has_per_share_security

this convention since it was added for a different reason:
In the API, the `Share` field name 'require_encryption'
was changed to 'encryption' in versions 8.1 and above.
This method was added in order to not break backward compatibility.

classes.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.ReplicaBinder

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.ReplicaBinder.replicate_volume
documentation:
Convenience wrapper around {func}`ReplicaBinder.replicate_entity`

```
seealso

```

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.ReplicaBinder.replicate_filesystem
documentation:
Convenience wrapper around {func}`ReplicaBinder.replicate_entity`

```
seealso

```

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.ReplicaBinder.replicate_cons_group
documentation:
Convenience wrapper around {func}`ReplicaBinder.replicate_entity`

```
seealso

```

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.ReplicaBinder.replicate_entity
documentation:
Replicates a entity or CG, creating its remote replica on the specified pool

```
param remote_pool

if omitted, remote_entity must be specified. Otherwise, means creating target entity

param remote_entity

if omitted, remote_pool must be specified. Otherwise, means creating based on
existing entity on target

param member_mappings

required if remote_entity is specified and is a consistency group. This parameter is
a dictionary mapping local member entities to remote ones
```

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.ReplicaBinder.replicate_entity_create_target
documentation:
Replicates an entity, creating its remote replica on the specified pool

```
param remote_pool

Remote pool to use for entity creation on the remote side

param remote_entity_names

A list or tuple containing the entity names created on remote side.
In case of cg - controls the entity names, not the cg name.
```

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.ReplicaBinder.replicate_entity_existing_target
documentation:
Replicates an entity, using a formatted/empty entity on the other side

```
param remote_entity

Remote entity to use for replication

param member_mappings

required if remote_entity is specified and is a consistency group. This parameter is
a dictionary mapping local member entities to remote ones
```

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.ReplicaBinder.replicate_entity_use_base
documentation:
Replicates an entity, using an existing remote entity and a base snapshot on both sides

```
param local_snapshot

Local base snapshot to use

param remote_snapshot

Remote base snapshot to use

param member_mappings

required if remote_entity is specified and is a consistency group. This parameter is a
dictionary mapping local member entities to tuples of (local_snapshot, remote_snapshot)
```

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.ReplicaBinder.replicate_entity_take_snap
documentation:
Replicates a entity, using the currently found data on both sides as a reference.

```
param entity

Local entity to use

param remote_entity

Remote entity to use

param member_mappings

required if remote_entity is specified and is a consistency group. This parameter is
a dictionary mapping local member entities to tuples of (entity, remote_entity)
```
Implements *system.replicas*

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.Replica.get_remote_replica

call the register_related_system method of the Infinibox object when a link to a remote system is constructed
for the first time

methods.name: infinisdk.infinibox.replica.Replica.get_remote_entity_pairs

Returns the entity_pairs configuration as held by the remote replica

this uses the remote command execution API to run the command over the inter-system
link
:::
